dimensions.rationale: The candidate is a single child of the stated baseline and limits implementation to Stage 6 plus narrow cumulative-validator and fixture compatibility changes; protected and later-stage work is explicitly excluded.

The review and execution evidence schemas are bound only to the baseline and accept self-declared PASS fields or source references; they do not bind the claimed observations to the candidate commit/tree or preserve independently verifiable command results.

Protected and production oracles are honestly marked NOT_RUN, but local advisory scans, container smoke, independent reviews and aggregate PASS counts are asserted without candidate-bound execution artifacts; the validator checks those assertions rather than establishing their provenance.

The candidate has the baseline as its sole parent, so reverting this one local commit restores the inspected baseline; the immutable diff contains no executed protected mutation requiring compensating rollback.

findings.finding: S6AC-006 cannot establish that two independent model families reviewed this candidate. The review schema requires only baseline_commit and mutable strings for reviewer_task/model_family, while the test merely counts two files, distinct string values and self-declared verdicts; candidate_commit, candidate_tree and request/diff digest are absent.
findings.required_fix: Version the review record schema to bind each record to the exact candidate commit/tree and immutable request or diff digest, validate those identities, and obtain fresh independent reviews against that exact target.

findings.finding: The committed local PASS evidence is assertion-only. The evidence schema accepts an arbitrary non-empty evidence_ref and PASS status, and validate_stage6 verifies those literals and aggregate counters without a candidate-bound command transcript, exit status or result digest. Consequently pip-audit, secret scan, container build/smoke, test counts and related PASS claims are not statically verifiable.
findings.required_fix: Produce sanitized machine-readable execution evidence bound to the candidate tree, exact command/tool versions, exit status and output digest for every claimed local gate; update the schema and validator to reject unbound or stale evidence, then re-review.

findings.finding: Capacity authorization evaluates only the existing snapshot. OperationalGate receives no prospective object size or repository/LFS/release delta, so a GREEN or YELLOW assessment can authorize an action whose next write crosses a hard platform or provisioned limit; the tests cover already-at-limit snapshots only.
findings.required_fix: Make write authorization include the proposed repository/LFS/object/release byte deltas, reject projected threshold or hard-limit crossings before invoking the callable, and add boundary tests for writes that cross limits from otherwise writable snapshots.

limitations: Static inspection did not execute tests, validators, dependency scans, container builds or workflows.
This review does not prove remote CI, protected Oracle behavior, real Gmail/private-repository behavior, production health or final AC-033.
The two disputed sibling review records were intentionally not inspected.
summary: FAIL: scope and mechanical rollback are acceptable, but candidate-bound evidence provenance is missing and the capacity gate cannot preflight prospective writes; all findings require remediation and fresh review.
